name: Civil Damages for Fraudulent Filing of Information Returns

eligibility.description: Applies when a third party willfully files a false information return; recovery includes costs to resolve resulting IRS deficiencies and potentially attorney fees.

actionability.retroactive_note: Action required during tax year — verify if still applicable

description: Taxpayers can sue for the greater of $5,000 or actual damages if someone willfully files a fraudulent information return (like a 1099 or W-2) regarding payments to them.
